items.title: Texans co-founder Janice McNair dies at age 89
items.body_text: Texans co-founder and senior chair Janice S. McNair died Tuesday in Houston at age 89, the team said.
items.author: DJ Bien-Aime

items.title: Police investigation of Jets' Smith no longer acti...
items.body_text: An investigation of Geno Smith for an incident in which a woman accused him of battery last month is no longer active, and the New York Jets quarterback will not be charged.

items.title: Cowboys' Snowden suspended 3 games by NFL
items.body_text: Cowboys outside linebacker Charles Snowden has been suspended for the first three games of the 2026 season, according to the NFL's transactions wire.

items.title: Rams' Jackson won't face felony charge for arrest
items.body_text: The Rams' Alaric Jackson will not face a felony charge after he was arrested on a domestic violence charge in June, the Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office confirmed to ESPN.
items.author: Sarah Barshop

items.title: Packers sign LB McDuffie to one-year extension
items.body_text: The Packers signed veteran linebacker Isaiah McDuffie to a contract extension Monday.

items.title: Free agent Diggs: No WR2 in NFL better than me
items.body_text: Free agent Stefon Diggs says he is better than any No. 2 wide receiver currently on an NFL roster.

items.title: Niners part owner agrees to purchase Seahawks
items.body_text: A group led by Niners limited partner Vinod Khosla has agreed to purchase the Seahawks, with sources telling ESPN the selling price will be a record $9.612 billion. The Khosla family will be required to divest its interest in the 49ers.
items.author: Seth Wickersham and Adam Schefter

items.title: No GPS tracker for Arnold, can work out for teams
items.body_text: Former Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold does not need to wear a GPS tracker as a condition of his release, a Florida judge ruled Friday, allowing him to continue to work out with NFL teams in an effort to get re-signed after being cut.
items.author: Xuan Thai

items.title: 🏈 Meet the family set to buy the Seahawks
items.body_text: Vinod Khosla is a prominent venture capitalist, and his wife, Neeru, is an education philanthropist.
